steps.title: Set up mass and silicon balance equations
steps.content:
Let $m_1$ = mass of the first piece (35% silicon) and $m_2$ = mass of the second piece (85% silicon).

The total mass equation is:
$$m_1 + m_2 = 60.0$$
For silicon content, the final sample is 55% silicon by mass, so it contains $0.55 \times 60.0 = 33.0$ grams of silicon. The silicon balance equation is:
$$0.35m_1 + 0.85m_2 = 33.0$$

steps.title: Solve for the mass of the second piece
Calculate silicon mass in the second piece
steps.content:
The mass of silicon in the second piece is 85% of its total mass:
$$0.85 \times 24.0 = 20.4 \text{ grams}$$
That confirms B is correct.
